description:
You are a performance engineer investigating the typical workload of a small demo application.
    A tiny SQLite database already exists on-disk.

    1. Location of the database  
       • Path: /home/user/projects/profile/data.db  

    2. Schema already present in the database  
       • Table name: metrics  
       • Columns and SQLite types (column order is fixed):  
         – id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
         – component   TEXT  
         – cpu_usage   REAL  

    3. Current rows in the metrics table  
       ┌────┬───────────┬──────────┐  
       │ id │ component │ cpu_usage│  
       ├────┼───────────┼──────────┤  
       │ 1  │ auth      │ 55.2     │  
       │ 2  │ api       │ 70.8     │  
       │ 3  │ db        │ 65.0     │  
       │ 4  │ ui        │ 40.0     │  
       └────┴───────────┴──────────┘  

    4. Your task  
       Use the sqlite3 command-line interface to calculate the arithmetic mean (AVG) of the cpu_usage column for every row in the metrics table and save that single numeric result to a plain-text report file.

    5. Report file requirements  
       • Destination directory: /home/user/profile_report/  
         (create the directory first if it does not yet exist)  
       • File name: avg_cpu_usage.txt  
       • Exact content (including capitalisation and punctuation):  
         Average CPU Usage: <value>  
         where <value> is the average printed with exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline character (ASCII LF).  
       Example of correct format (values will differ):  
         Average CPU Usage: 42.42⏎

    6. Constraints  
       • Achieve the calculation through a single sqlite3 invocation (no intermediate temporary files).  
       • Do not modify the database contents.  
       • No super-user rights are available; stay in /home/user.  
       • Feel free to create any additional directories inside /home/user if needed, but only /home/user/profile_report/avg_cpu_usage.txt will be graded.

    7. Validation hint  
       The automated test will:  
       a) Read /home/user/profile_report/avg_cpu_usage.txt  
       b) Confirm the line matches the pattern `^Average CPU Usage: [0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}\n$`  
       c) Parse the floating-point value and verify it equals the true average stored in the database (57.75) with two decimal places.  

    Use whatever bash commands you like, but remember that the whole task can be solved with one properly constructed sqlite3 CLI command.
truth: Average CPU Usage: 57.75
name: task_000000_7a8866ec
